macro_summary: Stocks edged higher Monday, with the S&P 500 holding near record levels and the Nasdaq rising 0.9%. Oil steadied at $101.81 as the Strait of Hormuz crisis enters its third month, while the US 10-year yield ticked down to 4.38%, easing some pressure on rate-sensitive sectors. The Fed's hold at 3.50–3.75% and persistent energy inflation keep rate-cut expectations pushed to late 2026.
commodities_note: WTI crude eased 0.1% to $101.81, remaining elevated on the ongoing Hormuz disruption. Gold slipped 0.7% to $4,610/oz, while the dollar index held at 98.19. The drop in US 10-year yield to 4.38% offers marginal relief to the Singapore bank cluster.

writeup.headline: ACCUMULATE — all gates pass on Goldman catalyst and R:R.

singapore.summary: The Singapore bank cluster remains under pressure from persistent US10Y above 4.20% and the strong SGD. DBS beat analyst forecasts, but the stock is overbought and blocked. OCBC and UOB hold near their 50‑day averages ahead of earnings this week, with NIM trajectory as the make‑or‑break metric.
singapore.key_development: DBS Group beat analyst forecasts last week — the only bank with YoY profit growth — but its RSI is now 71, hard‑blocking entry.

semiconductors.summary: Semis remain deeply bifurcated: ASML is the only name trading near its 50‑day with a workable R:R, while AMD, INTC, MU, TSM, NVDA, AVGO, and LITE are all extremely extended and hard‑blocked. SOXX added 1.7%, driven by the mid‑cap foundry names, but the mega‑cap overbought condition limits fresh entry.
semiconductors.key_development: AMD, INTC, MU, TSM, NVDA, AVGO, and LITE are all >10% above their 50‑day averages, with AMD +53% and INTC +79% — the cluster needs a broad pullback to become actionable.

big_tech.summary: BigTech is mixed: MSFT stabilized after the Azure OpenAI disclosure and offers a WATCH setup, while AMZN and GOOG are overbought and firmly on CAUTION. PLTR reports tonight with a 4‑prior‑earnings binary that could swing the stock 9–10% in either direction.
big_tech.thesis_status: Post‑earnings digestion; MSFT the lone watch
big_tech.key_development: Amazon and Alphabet remain extreme‑extension CAUTION after post‑earnings surges; PLTR’s after‑close report tonight is the next cluster catalyst.

active_risks: Iran‑US/Hormuz conflict: Strait remains disrupted despite April ceasefire; WTI ~$100+
US‑China tech decoupling: export controls on COMAC, LOGINK, and semiconductor equipment persist
AI capex bubble debate: $630B hyperscaler spend fuels inflation and rate‑cut uncertainty
geopolitical.market_impact: Elevated oil prices continue to compress Singapore bank NIMs and inflate input costs for semis. The rate‑pause environment keeps BigTech multiples under pressure, while defense‑AI names like PLTR benefit from geopolitical spending.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Stay selective; add WRD on the ACCUMULATE signal, but avoid chasing extended semis and BigTech until a pullback materializes or the May 7 FOMC decision offers rate clarity.

portfolio_snapshot.overall_stance: Cautiously bullish with one actionable name
portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: Hormuz-driven energy inflation and BigTech earnings bifurcation keep most positions in CAUTION; the only ACCUMULATE is a speculative robotaxi play with high upside but binary risk.

accumulate.note: Goldman Sachs Buy initiation validates thesis. R:R 2.0:1 to $8.33, stop below 50‑day SMA $7.30. Starter position — PAPER TRADE.
